properties.review: Another big one. Really cool layout to the bar. Runs along three little snugs.  Large smoking area. A bit too expensive though.

properties.name: Graingers
properties.review: Nice place. They serve either peas or beans.... So you've a fuck ton of choice.

properties.name: Kavanaghs
properties.review: Had to play hide and seek with the bar man for a little while but apart from that was grand. Plenty of room. Nice pints and SCAMPI FRIES.... YAY

properties.name: Merchants Arch
properties.review: What kind of a cunt hole can charge 14 euro for two bottles of piss and keep a straight face. Some poor fuckin tourist got quoted 85 quid for a single measure. Rip off central. Shall not be back...

properties.name: River Bar
properties.review: Meh... Over the hill hangout. And Kev reckons the barman was the cunt from Doyles

properties.name: Sweetmans
properties.review: Not a bad bar. Jacks are up 27 flights of stairs which is a bit annoyin and their own Stout is utter piss but the place is grand. Very central so gets a crowd.

properties.name: The Richmond
properties.review: Kev was a Meh... I was a little less impressed. Trying too much to be a restaurant kills the bar atmosphere. Not great tbh...

properties.name: The Geln of Aherlow
properties.review: I can't remember this at all. Kev assures me we did it though. Apparently the smoking ban was optional in there...

properties.name: McKennas
properties.review: I liked this pub. A few in watching the racing, barman offered to sell me tennis balls. Friendly locals....

properties.name: O'Loughlins
properties.review: Auld fart bar. Good old country bar look to the place. Jacks are out the back somewhere. Yappy enough punters

properties.name: The Lighthouse
properties.review: Kev was put off by all the board games. Bit expensive as well. But they had a pic of Phillo behind the bar so some saving grace at least. A little too hipster for the tastes though

properties.name: O'Neills
properties.review: Wouldnt be a massive fan. Nothing wrong with the place when we were in, it just seemed a little too nice. fancy carpets... fancy lighting... meh

properties.name: McLoughlins
properties.review: Must be the local celebrity hang out. Saw PJ Gallagher in there. Although the fact that he's Boez Scum has to drag the place down in terms of points. Drink was grand though

properties.name: Hardys
properties.review: As in Laurel and fuckin Hardy... This one is a bit controversial. First things first, it was a snobbish cunt hole that ya wouldnt feel comfortable drinking in anyways... But the controversial thing is that its basically a hotel bar. And the rules specifically state that hotel bars arent allowed. I wanted very much to exclude this place from the list but I checked the revenue site and apparently they are operating under a pub license, so... (shrug)

properties.name: Lanigans
properties.review: A regular of the Mammy. Not a bad bar. A load of shite on the walls for decoration and staff are generally friendly but they refused Kev after an Ireland match, so fuck ye. fuck ye in ur fuckin nancy pancy little arses.

properties.name: The Maple Tree
properties.review: Very friendly staff but smacks off a hotel. Drinks are too expensive and layout is too fancy. A big meh...

properties.name: The Blue Light
properties.review: Stunning views over Dublin from the many outdoor tables. Seems a good atmosphere in the boozer as well. Liked this place a lot. Although the roads on the way to it are fuckin tiny. Oh and they even had Nobbys Nuts. So extra bonus points.

properties.name: Smyths
properties.review: Oscar the barman has the best cross Chinese/Dub accent I've ever heard. Also Joe O'Shea wired the place and said they never paid him for it but fuck it at least the pint was nice

properties.name: Lamb Doyles
properties.review: I'd say the last time this place was done up was sometime in the 70's. Went with the kids to check out The Exploratorium beside it which just left me confused and scared. Food is shite. Bar is pretty good apart from that though. Friendly relaxed feel to the place. Very remote, so prob won't be back.

properties.name: Madigans
properties.review: Larger than you'd think from the outside. Nice local boozer. There was a one eyed dog asleep on the floor when we got in. Guiness wasn't the best but there was a lad actually working on the taps so that's my bad for going for a fuckin Guinness...

properties.name: The Foxhound Inn
properties.review: Like walking down a players tunnel to get into it. Big pub but the Sky box was busted so no racing on... Ended up having to talk to Kev... Ughhhh

properties.name: Cedar Lounge
properties.review: Good boozer. Friendly locals. They had the Dublin v Kerry game on repeat in the bar...

properties.name: Raheny Inn
properties.review: Pool hall upstairs if you're bored. Local feel to the place.... Scampi fries... they did have scampi fries... I do likes me scampi fries

properties.name: The Manhattan Beer Co
properties.review: Ginger Beer and some fuckin bitters addin thing... I dunno what the fuck Kev was drinking tbh. The place is modern and hipstery. Hog heads on the wall and animal hair backed bar stools... Although I'm sure the animal hair is fake. I can't imagine the hipsters would be for killing the poor animals and all that. A bit foody and full of auld ones but sure fuck it.. A pints a pint

properties.name: The Watermill
properties.review: Yeah good pub. Discout for OAPs which was kinda cool. Although I'm not an OAP. Good barman, good crowd in... decent boozer

properties.name: Horse and Hound
properties.review: Kev gave me some very solid advice here to not laugh at people when their horse comes last... He's a very wise zen master indeed.

properties.name: Pipers Corner
properties.review: Chipboard walls. 3 staff on to cater for the 6 people in the bar. More gin than ya cud shake a fist at... All a bit hipstery.. Not bad though

properties.name: Sackville Louge
properties.review: Small but good. Warm feel to the place. Barman was good and chatty. And there was a lad in swapping door handles for pints... Love that...

properties.name: Briodys
properties.review: Normal down to earth bar. Nothing special. Barman on was handling the place well. Signed McManamon montage on the wall look kinda cool. More pissers than the DunLeary/Rathdown head office...

properties.name: Madigans
properties.review: Ok pub. Seemed a bit touristy. This is where things start to go south though. Sure who wants to go see fuckin Bulgaria anyways...

properties.name: Grand Central
properties.review: Ughhh... Wasn't mad about it. Bit too nice... or whatever. They had lights on the table that when turned upside down electrocuted the heroin addicted barstaff though. So that was kinda fun to play with

properties.name: Madigans
properties.review: Once Kev wanted a breakfast from Annes... But Annes wasnt open... So he went in here and had a pint instead... Oh the hilarity of it all... Laugh Laugh Laugh.... Seriously though... Shite!

properties.name: Brannigans
properties.review: Zap wasn't in which was a bit dissapointing but apart from that tis a standard city centre pub. A load of food orders going out which is meh.. but pints and service was generally good...

properties.name: The Silver Penny
properties.review: A weatherspoons pub. Was hooping when we were there and food and drink are piss cheap. It's hard not to like these places... :shrug:

properties.name: The Gondola
properties.review: Recently done up so was looking very well. Very large food area out the back but still a well seperated bar at the front. Bar is small enough but had a good atmosphere with a few people in for the match. Seems to be a small bar only area out in the lounge as well which might suit people also. Good parking available here too... Good shout for a Sunday lunch out with the kids or whatever... Kev said the bargirls in the lounge were cracking as well although I was only served by Eamonn... so...

properties.name: The Salmon Leap
properties.review: Was our first post Covid world pub. To be honest we didn't even know the place was in Dublin until someone highlighted the stupidity of the localized lockdowns being placed along county lines... Cool pub though. They put the effort in to make social distancing possible. Ya can rent a horse box to have ur 9 euro meal and few scoops, which sounds a lot worse than it actually is. After nearly 5 months without a pint in the pub, the Guinness here was possibly the best Guinness I've ever had. Fuckin loved it. Kev only had the one though. He was driving. Aghgghhhahahagahagahagahagahhahahahhahaahhahhahhahahahahaahahaha...

properties.name: The Balrothery Inn
properties.review: These are strange covid times we're livinig in. So hard to rate a place. First pub we've been to in a year.... Staff were bang on anyways. Only had the one here so not much to say. Layout is grand. Wayyyyy out for us. Only reason we made it here was by bringing the kids out to Ardgillan castle for a trip. Defo not the worst though.

properties.name: The Strawberry Hall
properties.review: Impressive outdoor setup. Lots of parking space which is handy as the place is a bit out of the way. Savage views of the underside of the M50 are unique I guess.

properties.name: The Wrens Nest
properties.review: Beautiful looking bar. Will definitely be back as we had to drink outside due to covid. Quiet bar, good vibes and they did a savage little blueberry cocktail for Kev.

properties.name: The Celt
properties.review: Gary, the barman, does not give a fuck what you do. Nice pub, good decor, very music oriented. Good mix of tourists and locals as well.

properties.name: Riot
properties.review: Friendly barman, a little bit hipstery. Lots of deals on shots and stuff like that. Great location but prob geared for a younger crowd than me

properties.name: The Wild Duck
properties.review: Looks like a hotel bar. Loads of boats hanging out of the ceiling for some reason. Was pretty good though. Kev flip-flopped on the place a bit but the cycling motif in the jacks really upper the tierage.

properties.name: The Portehouse
properties.review: Bargirl definitely had a thing for me here. In another life it could've been magical. What can I say, the place is a purveryor of porters and lots of other stuff. Great selection but steep prices

properties.name: BrewDog
properties.review: What a pile of wank this place is. I don't know if it's the fuckin 7.50 pints or the stupid beards and hats. Feels anything like a pub. The beer was watery and shite or else made from the anal drippings of a rare albino tree ferret.... ya know.... just to be cool.... fuckin waste of space.

properties.name: John Clarke & Sons
properties.review: Decent selection of beers on tap. Great base for a pre lansdowne drink. A little bit on the pricey side but sure blame the fuckin tax on that. Also tried Islands Edge for the first time here, It won't be replacing me Guinness. No salt & vinegar though. WTF is that about?

properties.name: Gravediggers
properties.review: Have to admit I was a little bit overawed in here. Massive reputation. Of course went with the Guinness and Coddle. Bang on. No complaints. Outside of that, they have a savage little menu. Really want to try the bacon and cabbage spring roll.... If you're reading this, and yer not me, just fuckin go here and do a tour of the Glasnevin cementary and then sink a few Guinness and have some coddle and batch. Fuckin perfect things dont happen often.

properties.name: The Brian Boru
properties.review: Apart from the savage name it's not a bad little boozer. Not cheap but fuckin where is these days. No complaints. Even if itis in the heart of Boez Scum land.

properties.name: The Berndar Shaw (Part Deux)
properties.review: Ok let's be honest for a sec. I'm too old for shiholes like this. It's not the beanie hats. It's not the fact that the bar staff are fuckin young enough to play with me kids. It's not even the stupid fuckin disco ball swirling around the bar at fuckin 2 o'clock on a Wednesday. It's just... not a fuckin bar. Let it off to the young wans. Fuck it. I'll resign meself as an unknowing dinosaur in this sense.

properties.name: Quinlans - The Black Lion
properties.review: Massive pub. was in with the kids for a bit of a lunch. Scampi was bang on. Guinness was nice and slurpy. Hard to rate a place when it's midday but nothing to object about anyways.

properties.name: Rascals
properties.review: Doesn't really feel like a pub and they've no fuckin crisps but I won't hear a bad word said about the place. They support Pat so I support them with my drunken endeavours. Also the pizza looked nice.

properties.name: The Black Forge Inn
properties.review: Owned by a scumbag. And they don't have crisps.

properties.name: Eleanoras
properties.review: Auld farts feel to the place. I like it. For some reason they didn't seem to want me to pay. Kev said mention that they had very small bags of nuts. But they didnt charge for them anyways, so can't gripe.

properties.name: The Halfway House
properties.review: Massive place. Modern gastropub feel to the place. Watched the first half of Ireland v Portugal here. Why not at the match you ask? Because every muppet in the country wanted to lick the sweat off Ronaldos balls. Fuck em. Let them at it. I was happy enough drinking instead

properties.name: The gate Bar
properties.review: Was well oiled by the time I got here so can't remember too much about it. Kev recognised the barman from The Black Lion. Also he was very adamant that the wrong drink was just a mistake. Grand boozer. No complaints.

properties.name: Urban Brewing
properties.review: Meh. Nice selection of beers and downstairs looks kinda cool but it doesn't really feel like a pub. Nothing bad about it but just not my style.

properties.name: Cafe Insane
properties.review: Oh god. Do not like. Kev enjoyed the singing. fuck it... its done... i never have to go back

properties.name: Mulligan & Haines
properties.review: Small bar but seemed popular. Nothing overly memorable about it but nothing wrong with it either

properties.name: The Norseman
properties.review: Very touristy but the prices weren't ridiculously higher than other places. Maybe everywhere in Dublin is just a rip off anyways. If ya don't mind dodging coked up tourists then it's fine... I guess...

properties.name: Farmer Browns
properties.review: Fairly standard housing estate type pub. A good few in for Sunday lunch. Lots of staff buzzing around. No complaints though. We were in after doing a little hike in the mountains there. Its a nice area.

properties.name: Dockers
properties.review: Pretentious overpriced kip. Fuckin 4.50 for nuts. Yis arent fuckin New Yrok. FUck off will ya.

properties.name: The South Strand
properties.review: Another fuckin Weatherspoons. I really want to hate these places so much. Soul-less. But fuck me they do some perfectly priced drink. Stopped in on the way to a match in Lansdowne and the previous pub, the round was 14, this place the round is 8. Like how can ya argue with that. Even if it is an assembly line hammer blow to real locals... conflicted...

properties.name: 57 The Headline
properties.review: Good chat at the bar. Barman was from Mayo and a punter was a closet Combat 18 fan so plenty of sporty banter. Place looks more like a restaurant than a pub but they have Ambush on tap (which is fuckin heavenly) and good chat == happy punters

properties.name: Leonards Corner
properties.review: Looks like a good local bar. Was quiet when we were there. Cheaper than its neighbour but nothing massively standoutish about it.

properties.name: MVP
properties.review: Same owner/operator as Square Ball and very much the same vibe. Barman was a god yap though. He's the first lad to actually ask for a link to the review site, so hello if yer reading. In terms of the place, its a bit pricey and the furniture is a bit poncey. I'm sure we're not their target audience but a decent drink selection on tap, so no complaints.

properties.name: Harold House
properties.review: Old bar feel to the place. Football was on the TV. No fuckin salt and vinegar though. WTF. Plenty of Dubs licence plates on the wall so say it'd be a good shout for watching a match.

properties.name: The Elphin Bar
properties.review: Nice bar. Plenty of room. Most importantly though it was open by half 11 on a bank holiday Monday unlike all the kips in Howth... :D

properties.name: Moss Lane
properties.review: Some expensive-ass whiskey in the place but has a nice feel to it. Little snug in the corner as well. Owner apparently has another place out in Donabate but it wasn't Oscars place...

properties.name: The Beer Market
properties.review: Standard enough Galway brewhouse type deal. Beers on a chalk board. Lots of wooly hats for no discernable reason. The window at the front of the bar is a savage little view out on the world though. Nice to sink a pint and stare at randos walking by

properties.name: The Clock
properties.review: Real local bar but the owner is a Pats fan so immediately in the good books. Scarf up behind the bar. Schlurpy pints. No complaints

properties.name: Dudleys
properties.review: Ambush on tap. Fuck yeah. Bargirl confused Kev though... Another great cross Brazilian/Dublin accent

properties.name: Johns Bar & Haerdashery
properties.review: We were all set to hate this place. Made up to look like an old shop and then suddenly morphs into a fuckin American diner in the middle and then the jacks at the back. Seems to be putting a lot of effort in to look like something but it wasnt too bad tbh. Good crowd in for pre-gig drinks so the atmosphere was alright

properties.name: Tom Kennedys
properties.review: Standard enough place. Some world class Darts lads warming up. Or maybe they were shit. Da Fuck do I know about Darts. Another one that gets a bit of atmosphere from pre gig crowds so there's an interesting mix in the place

properties.name: The Lock Keeper
properties.review: Nice location alongside the canal. Nice food and reasonably priced.

properties.name: Meaghers
properties.review: Tis a magical wonderous place where bartenders use some kind of vulcan mind meld power to guess what crisps you secretly desire most and your first round is always free. We were even joined by two Mexican lads Francisco and Alvaro. Unfortunately they forgot to bring the Tortillas... much sadness...

properties.name: Bowes
properties.review: So lets be clear from the get go. It may be owned by the same owner as a certain kip hole cunt place.... BUT ITS A DIFFERENT BAR. Dodgey gray area sure. Bar is nice though. Francis joined us for a pint but he was a little bit scared of all the old women wandering around stealing glasses. Still... Pints were schlurpy

properties.name: Bridge Tavern
properties.review: Brilliant Dubs mural means ya can spot this place from a mile away. Tunes from the flats were blasting and the place was hopping before the Dubs Kerry game. Fast bar service, brilliant weather. Had a good time here...

properties.name: Penny Hill
properties.review: Big roomy middle estate style pub. Things to like and dislike so could go either way. Was busy when we were there, was a cracking day so lots sitting outside for food. Plenty of staff on and the bar staff seemed decent but one of the lounge boys knicked me change... 🤣. Not the usual style of bar I like but nothing seriously wrong with it tbh

properties.name: The Finches
properties.review: Bit of a kip this tbh. Nothing wrong with the place just old and mostly unmaintained. Atmosphere iside was grand. Its big and roomy but stick some new fuckin padding in the couches

properties.name: Hudson Shithole
properties.review: I really wanted to exclude this place from the list in the hope that it was a restaraunt licence or something but I was too lazy to check. Fuckin wank stain of a place anyways. Avoid.

properties.name: The Viscount
properties.review: The bar appears to be named after a plane which crashed while flying from Cork to London in the 60s. Was quiet when we were here but the decor was pretty cool. No complaints. Seems a good local.

properties.name: The Pound & The Attic
properties.review: First bar that we did in Swords and tbh, we peaked early. Prob the best boozer in the town. Barman was very friendly, helped us plan the route for the rest of the pubs. Bar was a nice feel. Would defo return.

properties.name: The Old Schoolhouse
properties.review: Absolutely massive place. Completely devoid of any kind of atmosphere though. No charger behind the bar either... and there probably was a charger behind the bar. Also, why fuckin play CHristmas songs over the football commentary. Not a fan.

properties.name: The Old Borough
properties.review: Nearly didnt do this place as we thought it was a hotel but a quick scan of the revenue site confirmed they have a 7 day pub licence so it counts. Yes its a Widdershins but tbh it's better than the Schoolhouse place behind it. Also 20 quid for two dinners and two pints, can't fuckin argue with that. And the barman gave me a charger for the phone as well. No complaints

properties.name: Wrights Cafe Bar
properties.review: Got a bang of auld lad trying to pick up young wans from this place. Seems to be disco bar style. Not a massive fan but nice beers on tap at least.

properties.name: The Arch Bar
properties.review: Not a bad bar. Good staff, plenty of viewing angles for the TV but still places to hide away from the TV. Decent crowd in for a Tuesday. Would use the place again for a bit of food but very big so doesnt have that cozy bar feel.

properties.name: The Cock Tavern
properties.review: It's like a seven mile trek from the front bar down to the jacks. Maybe third best pub in the town. Nothing against it though. Except the fact they don't have salt & vinegar crips. WTF. Anyways, beer was nice.

properties.name: Hole In The Wall
properties.review: Popped in for a quick pint in the Presidents snug after bursting James' nose open with an over-zealous challenge in the Phoenix Park. Crisps and coke solve all woes though. Great bit of history in the place. Would have loved to stay for another few.

properties.name: Bru House
properties.review: Same as all the Galway Bay pubs, there's a great selection of beers but the prices are definitely steep. Nice feel to the place though, not too hipster. Nice old library in one of the corners. Fairview park across the road is great for a play as well so the pub works well after you've built up a thirst.

properties.name: Gaffneys
properties.review: Great little snug at the front of the bar. Nice classic feel to the place. Liked this place, would defo be back. Close enough to let the kids play in the park as well... :D

properties.name: Cherrytree
properties.review: Exciting enough visit with Spurs going 5 nil down to Newcastle inside 20 mins. But the free pastries perked Kev back up a bit. Nice pub. Plenty of space and a good local feel.

properties.name: Kestrel
properties.review: Very large and was full of people getting their carvery on while we were there. Wouldnt be a massive fan. Nearly too large to feel like a proper pub but no major complaints against it.

properties.name: The Old Mill
properties.review: We were doing a couple pubs and I was driving so switched onto a zero after a bottle. Barman looked absolutely disgusted with me. TBH that's prob a plus point for the place. Nice local feel. Nothing against it

properties.name: Ahernes
properties.review: Nice roomy bar. Plenty of room in the place and a good smattering of TVs. Kev was mucho impressed with the lemony scent in the jacks. He's an odd fuck though.

properties.name: The Hideout
properties.review: Savage location for the bar, tucked away in a tiny little housing estate just around the corner from Croker. Got in for a quick pint before the Kilkenny v Clare semi final. Friendly staff, bit of banter, great location.... 3 thumbs up.

properties.name: Ryans Hogan Stand
properties.review: Gets a decent crowd in on a match day. Does be absolutely wedged. Staff still churn out the pints though. Always a joy to watch quality bar staff in action

properties.name: Kennedys
properties.review: Brought Emma and Jenny here for some grub after a camogie blitz in UCD. Fuck me the place was expensive. Nothing wrong with apart. The food was nice, the staff were nice, the beer was fine.... Just the prices in pubs now if feckin mental

properties.name: The Millhouse
properties.review: Was a flying visit. Hard to rate. Fuckin expensive. But sure every fuckin place seems to be expensive now. Plenty of staff. Quick service. No real complaints. Just.... expensive

properties.name: An Poitin Stil
properties.review: Lots of old antique stuff and a well run bar. Gets a good crowd in and they have people managing parking etc. Prob an interesting spot to show off to people. Can't imagine that it'd ever feel like a regular to me.

properties.name: Pillar Bar
properties.review: Decent tunes in the place. Good selection of beers. Bit expensive but ya can't really get more central with the location. Not really a bar feel. More disco bar / lounge type deal but nothing wrong with the place I suppose. Barman had very shakey hands.

properties.name: Cassidys
properties.review: Real Berlin style grunge bar look. Meanies and burger buns crisps. Not fuckin bad. Barman had hair like the dude from Static-X as well. Cool bar. Daycent beers.

properties.name: The Confession Box
properties.review: Tiny little bar. real mix of locals and blow ins. Lovely slurpy Guinness. I likes it

properties.name: McDaids
properties.review: Able to spy on the fancy lookin cars parking up in the Westbury. Pub is grand, the trek to jacks can be lethal if you've had a few. Honest clientelle though, left me laptop here after a drink and only realised two pubs later and it was still there. Bonus points for that

properties.name: Nearys
properties.review: All the barmen are wearing fancy dicky bos. And Kev spotted Bo Martin at the bar. Who's Bo Martin you ask? Shane Lowrys ex caddy. Why ex? Fuck knows, apparently Lowry is a bit of a prick with the caddies. Sure what do I know. Pub is grand anyways. Did I mention they wear dicky bos?

properties.name: Sheehans
properties.review: They've a wall of whiskey bottles locked behind little doors. Reminded me of that second wizard of oz film where yer one had a lot of heads locked in little cabinets. Anyways pub was fine. Upstairs is more restauranty but no complaints.

properties.name: Swan Bar
properties.review: Has a statue of some Rugger Bugger in the corner. Bar was grand. Nice old wooden bar. No complaints but nothing standoutish

properties.name: The Landmark
properties.review: Had a few tunes going and the bar sang happy birthday to some lad and he was scarlet. Also took me way longer than it should have to find the jacks. No complaints though, nice bar.

properties.name: Whelans
properties.review: Went to see a Pat McManus gig here... but there was no Pat McManus... Where the fuck was Pat McManus????

properties.name: Ryans
properties.review: Fuck me I'm old...

properties.name: Noctors
properties.review: Lovely auld one behind the bar. Real local place. First time in a long time that I've been in a Dublin pub that was cash only. Friendly locals though and reasonably priced pint. The Guinness was fuckin top notch too.

properties.name: The Fourth Corner
properties.review: Hipstery type place. Decent selection of beers but doesn't have that homely pub feel to the place

properties.name: Fallons (The Capstan)
properties.review: I like it. Good classic bar feel. Was busy enough. Ling used to live in the area and its near the best named Guitar shop ever. What's not to like

properties.name: Tenters
properties.review: A bit meh. Felt like a hotel lobby. It did have a cool old map of DUblin on the wall. I liked that. Also the barman ran out after me when I fogot me cap so nice peoples. Just the atmosphere was a bit meh. They seemed to have take home dinners though which was kinda novel

properties.name: The Liberty Belle
properties.review: Nice local pub feel to the place. I don't really remember it well tbh. Guinness was taking it's toll at this stage

properties.name: Luckys
properties.review: More of a restaurant feel than a bar. But sure fuck it, its done now

properties.name: The Greedy Eagle
properties.review: Nothing particularly against the place. It's an expensive part of Dublin, so was prepared for the prices. Its big and roomy. It just doesn't have a pub vibe tbh. More restauranty. No complaints though. Had a pint. The darts were on. Grand place.

properties.name: Fitzgeralds
properties.review: If I ever win the lotto and move to this part of Dublin, this would prob be me local. I liked it. Decent selection of pints on tap. Good bar feel. Also, the jacks handle are made out of old pennies which was pretty cool. Not a bad pub.

properties.name: Walters
properties.review: What an absolute waste of space. Staff were shite, couldn't pull a pint to save their lives and absolutely reeked of lack of interest. Decor was crap. Clientelle was boring. Just a really shit pub. Only reason we stayed in the kip so long was because it took so long to get served. It wasn't even fuckin busy. Useless kip.

properties.name: Buck Mulligans
properties.review: It's a weird fuckin layout. They've stupid fuckin low tables in the middle of the walkways. Big sofa style chairs. Only one person in the jacks at a time. Just weird. Prob be a decent boozer with a better interior design. But meh.... Seems to be one for the hipsters

properties.name: The Purty Kitchen
properties.review: They had a staff to customer ratio of about 27 to 1 and there was like 20 customers in. But apart from that, it was fine. Introduced Kev to yellow spot. No complaints though. Grand pub.

properties.name: The Coach House Pub
properties.review: Lovely ornate interior. A library wall is actually a great idea and something I'd love to see more of. It's an expensive area, so ya pay for it but definitely one of the more impressive look & feels to a pub

properties.name: Ballinteer House
properties.review: Big modern gastro pub feel for it. No great enjoyment off the interior and not exactly cheap. Nothing particularly against the place but most likely will never frequent it.

properties.name: Gormleys Pub
properties.review: Lovely rustic local. Raging fire in the corner. Quiet day time drink. Only a few lads and the racing. If I was living in the middle of fuckin no-where, this is the type of pub I'd want as me local.

properties.name: The Brock Inn
properties.review: Was there with the kids so was in the lounge for food. Lovely grub. Busy place, does a good trade. There's a pnp course beside it which me Da loves and it's right in the path for Dublin airport so nice for any airplane spotters.

properties.name: Walshs
properties.review: Great pub. Was heaving on a Saturday evening. Has a cool little front bar as well. Also.... Spicy Nuts... Fuck Yeah

properties.name: The Belfry
properties.review: Slightly weird American cocktail bar feel to the place but it's not too bad. Decent 70s playlist on and the Guinness was fine. Was starting to grow on Kev by the end

properties.name: The Glimmer Man
properties.review: One of the few pubs in Dublin that's cash only. I guess it's a hipster thing. There wasn't a fuckin inch of wall space that didnt have some poster or milk float or suitcase. Just seemed to be random. Not sure what effect they were going for. Also.... The Guinness was shite. Watery. Just badly pured tbh. There was a good crowd in though

properties.name: Mulligans
properties.review: Hipster bar. Big fuckin beard on the Cark barman, Locally produced ales, People sitting at the bar playing D&D... Nothing wrong with the place but quite pricey and not my scene

properties.name: Tommy O'Garas
properties.review: Had everything going for it.... Except the fuckin Boez jersey on the wall. Nice bar. Meant to be a good trad sesh in the place as well but there was no music when we were there.

properties.name: Oscars
properties.review: I feel harsh saying this because there was nothing really bad about the place but it's everything that's wrong with modern trendy pubs. The staff are pretty useless, the drink is expensive(ish), it feels more like a restaurant by asking you to wait for a table (there were like 3 fuckin occupied tables when we were in). Decor was shite as well. Oh and they refuse tracksuits, I always find that a bit snobby too.

properties.name: Token
properties.review: One of the most interesting non-local type pubs that I've been too. We made a little effort to get here seeing as the pub is closing down. The place was filled with arcard games and pinball machines. Decent selection of beers as well (although the Ambush was out... arrrggghhhhh). Kev kicked my ass in Street Fighter. He picked Ken. He always fuckin picks Ken. Anyways, enjoyable as something different. Sad to see it shutting down.

properties.name: The Summit Inn
properties.review: More restaurant than pub. Yet they don't do breakfasts. Why not do breakfasts. All the constituent parts were in the menu just allow them to be added to a plate. Inside looks kinda nice and the chowder was good but not the place you'd go for a sesh

properties.name: McNeills
properties.review: Yeah nice proper boozer feel to the place. Was prob the best pub that we visited on the day in Howth. Pool table, good barman... Would prob be me local if I lived there

properties.name: O'Connells
properties.review: Fuckin useless American tourist focussed wank stain of a place. Every table has a reserved sign on it by default. Just not a welcoming pub feel. Didn't like it

properties.name: Findlater
properties.review: Meh. Another fuckin tourist experiment more than a pub. Shit staff. Shit drinks. Shit atmosphere. Just avoid if yer looking for a drink

properties.name: The Bloody Stream
properties.review: What can ya say about the best pub in the world ever (well.. in Fingla in 2015)... It's not actually that bad. The owner seemed drunk and his idiot roided up son seemed a little retarded but it had a nice open fire. And the drinks were grand. Borderline pub/restaurant vibes again but not as bad as O'Connels or Findlater. Enjoyed the pint so no mad complaints.

properties.name: The Bayside Inn
properties.review: Ah this place is a little gem. Good access to TVs. Good b6356arman. Good pints. Even a fuckin buffalo skull on the wall. WTF. Would defo drink here if I lived in the area.

properties.name: Palmerstown House
properties.review: Massive pub. Loads of room. We were there early for a bit of lunch with the kids and was eerily empty but I'd say it's good when there's a crowd in.

properties.name: The Silver Granite
properties.review: Looks a bit run down from teh front and was dead when we were there except for two eegits arguing about riding each others sisters. Looks big though. I'd say it's better with a crwd in.

properties.name: Clearys
properties.review: Great price for a pint. One of the cheapest in Dublin. Savage little Mexican place (that's defo more expensive though) next door and ya can eat it in the pub. Great option for a pint and a taco before a Pats game.

properties.name: Duffys
properties.review: Large and modern. Bar staff were a bit shit. But was a bit of craic watching the Milton hurricane buildup on the TV. Most likely wouldn't bother with the palce if it was me local

properties.name: Fowlers
properties.review: Best bar in Malahide. Savage little snug in the place. Also, Kev didnt know who was the 2008 Gold Cup winner.... And he says he likes horses... <eye-roll>

properties.name: Gilbert & Wright
properties.review: Meh... Not great tbh. Looks more like a nightclub. Also, the weird shape of the wall suggests that maybe some people have been killed and hidden in the walls there

properties.name: O'Dwyers
properties.review: Only bar in Portmarnock I think. It was grand. A goo spot for plane watching actually. Also, they had YunYun rings at the bar!

properties.name: The White House
properties.review: Savage bar. Lovely range going in the corner. Nice friendly fella handing out beer mats. Would defo drink here again

properties.name: Racecourse Inn
properties.review: Nothing wrong with the bar. Seemed a few in, was a good buzz in the place but the building is one of them altogether type shop things and looks a bit shit.

properties.name: Disndat
properties.review: Kudos for being the cheapest pint of Guinness in Dublin Monday to Friday between 1 and 4. Head on the Guinness was a bit manky tbh but we'll cut them some slack as it was prob the first pour of the day. Pint and a sangidg for 10 euro. Not bad

properties.name: The Snug
properties.review: A real local boozer. Good characters in the place. Dont mind the lad that was doing his methdaone beside us

properties.name: Kavanghs
properties.review: Grand auld local. A few lads propping up the bar. Bit of birthday cake doing the rounds. Would have no problem drinking here

properties.name: Peader Browns
properties.review: Ooh Ah Up the Ra say Ooh Ah Up the Ra... Also, they sell vintage jerseys in the hall

properties.name: Peggy Kellys
properties.review: Lovely big spacious place. Nice fire going. Just fuckin dead. Pricey as well.

properties.name: McGowans
properties.review: Is it a Chinese, is it a pub, is it a Golf memorial.... Who knows... Who cares.... Was grand

properties.name: Lysters
properties.review: Nothing wrong with it. Bit of sport on the telly. Pizza available if so inclined. Pints flowing. Nothing remarkable but nothing bad

properties.name: The Stone Boat
properties.review: Officially pub number 400... So it'll always have that.

properties.name: The Igo Inn
properties.review: I always maintain that the barman makes the pub and the Igo is the eiptomy of that. Fine roomy pub, nothing particularly special but the barman was bang on. Popped in for a couple of scoops and bit of food with the kids after climbing Kiliney Hill. Barman was bang on. Guinness was lovely. Would def return if it wasnt a million miles away from me.

properties.name: The Yacht
properties.review: Expensive gastro pub with kids working instead of barmen. Really not a great experience for a pint. Expensive too. Shan't be back daaarling

properties.name: Connollys The Sheds
properties.review: Pretty expensive but maybe understandable for the area. Seems an alright bar. There was a funeral starting when we were there so maybe not the best time to rate but wouldnt have a problem drinking here again..... if the drink was bought for me.....

properties.name: Sin é
properties.review: Dark and trendy vibe to the place. There was a chess club in having a games night when we were there. I think I'd like the place but it's fuckin expensive. 16:60 for two pints is mad.

properties.name: Darkie Kellys
properties.review: Very much targeted at Americans. Not that it's bad though. Good sport on. Nice session going in the corner. Looked to have some savage food. But it's priced to match all that. Apparently there are local prices but it wasn't offered to me no matter how much I bemoaned the Dubs losing to Tyrone

properties.name: The Giddy Dolphin
properties.review: Massive place. Lots of game options (pool, digital darts) and even a wow burger in the place. TBH, it doesn't feel like a pub. More an event space. Another one that's quite pricey.

properties.name: Annes Bar & Hardware
properties.review: Ughhh. This is just everything that's wrong with pubs in Dublin atm. Stupid faux interior made to look like an old hardware store. Staff that don't give a fuck. ZERO atmospohere. And expensive drinks. Just..... Shite.....

properties.name: Fitzsimons
properties.review: I need to compose meself here. Yes, yer in Temple Bar and the rent is prob high. Yes, you've stuck on some shit guitarist during the day. Yes, you have more fuckin bar stafff than rats in New York. But No. Just fuckin NO WAY can you justify charging twenty one fuckin euro for two pints. I was just lost for words. Fuckin insanity

properties.name: Gills
properties.review: Nearest bar to Croke park. And by fuck do they do some trade when there's game on. Respect to the barmen here.

properties.name: Memas
properties.review: It's a bit hipstery but it was alright tbh. An attempt to gentrify the area. A lot of space around the back and smelt like the food was nice

properties.name: Morris bar
properties.review: We thought we'd found the cheapest bar in the Dublin till we realised the barman just forgot to charge for the nuts. Still not the worst in terms of prices. Small enough place and Kev got accosted by some drunk one complaining about someone taking a shit in the snug but sure all part of the fun.

properties.name: The Park West Tavern
properties.review: Big gastro pub in the middle of an industrial estate so never gonna have that homely pub feel. Nothing wrong with the place though. Lots of staff on. Quick service. Bar man seemed a friendly fella

properties.name: O'Sheas The 79 Inn
properties.review: Big roomy pub. Loads of music stuff on the walls which was kinda cool but the most impressive thing by far was that they had NOBBYS NUTS. Fuck yeah.

properties.name: Downeys
properties.review: Bit of a bomb shelter this one. Apparently me Das favourite pub in Ballyer though. Was v quiet when there but nothing wrong with the place.

properties.name: The Manhattan
properties.review: An upstairs pub. Now that's a novelty. Massive bar to be fair. I'd say it used to be downstairs as well but there's a Chinese in there now

properties.name: Tim Younges
properties.review: Reminded me of The Roost in Maynooth with the shape of the bar. Kev was dazzling the lad sitting next to him with his galactical knowledge of Womens Tennis. Fucker just wouldn't take a hint...

properties.name: Chasers
properties.review: Another upstairs pub in Ballyer. Comfy place, nothing wrong with it but nothing particularly special either

properties.name: The Villager
properties.review: Lovely small homely feel to this bar. Althoguh the barman seemed to be a coke fiend. And they had the ginger beer and bitters for Kev so three thumbs up

properties.name: The Mullingar House
properties.review: Who's round is it? Kevs? Throw on a bowl of wings onto the round. Gettin hungy now. Really wanted to get into the rampart thingy but didnt know how. Brain wasn't functioning. Also, it's a bitch of a walk down to Heuston from there. Who's stupid idea was that.

properties.name: Oldtown House
properties.review: Very remote so can't see meself being back but it's a lovely pub. Roaring fire in the corner, lots of sport memorabilia on the walls. A mug collection overhanging the bar and a barman who will serve you when the race is done.... so wait yer turn.... Oh and the stained glass GAA windows are cool. Would defo be a regular if I was local.

properties.name: The Milestone
properties.review: Great little boozer. Lots of racing TVs setup. Bit of a crowd in even at 12 in the day. Talking about Might Mouse but fuck it at least there were people. Big roomy pub.

properties.name: Mrs O's
properties.review: I can't remember the last pub that had a snooker table in it, never mind two. Massive place. Was very quiet when we were there though.

properties.name: Dempseys
properties.review: Nice comfy little bar. Friendly staff and patrons. No complaints about this place. A lad at the bar was pissed already at 1 o'clock.

properties.name: Hamlet Bar
properties.review: Fuckin miles away from the town. Tis the typical centre of a housing estate type bar. Loads of room, doesnt really feel like a bar from the outside but nothing wrong with the place. Except the location.

properties.name: The Harvest
properties.review: We fucked up here. Went into the lounge by mistake. Looked like a plush fancy yoke. All cushions and shit. Should've went into the bar. But... There was a drunk lad who stores his wine behind the bar.... at elast there was him...

properties.name: Joe Mays
properties.review: Beautiful location. Barman was sound as well. Had a good yap. Nothing special inside but felt like a bar so we were happy enough.

properties.name: Blue Bar
properties.review: Tis basically a restaurant but it has a bar licence so it had to be done. More staff than customers when we were there and while I don't think I'll be backl, there was nothing really wrong with the place. Just no bar feels.

properties.name: Stoop Your Head
properties.review: Another restaurant but had a bar licence. Meh is the best I can say...

properties.name: The Bus Bar
properties.review: Big tall American lad behind the bar. Always seems a little awkward. A few in, not a bad atmosphere. Barman served one pint through the front door for some fuckin reason.

properties.name: The Gladstone Inn
properties.review: Kevs favourite pub of the crawl. Bunch of retards sitting at the bar and cash only... What's not to like.

properties.name: Nealons Bar
properties.review: Cosy little snug in front of a fire. Felt a little snobbish but was grand

properties.name: The Coast Skerries Inn
properties.review: Nice layout to the place. Circular bar type thing. The fat bald lad (no not me) was nearly fighting with a bunch of yung lads. Think he was protecting a yung wans honour or something. But all was good in the end.

properties.name: The Waterside
properties.review: Massive place. Even the bar has tns of space but still retains that local feel. If yer going for a walk along the canal it's a great stopping off point and the Pattaya restaurant beside it has some savage food for the soakage.

properties.name: Franks Bar
properties.review: Basically just a waiting room for FX Buckleys restaurant. Nothing wrong with the place, very well maintained etc but no local vibes

properties.name: The Wicked Wolf
properties.review: Barman was nice and frindly and seemed a decent atmosphere inside. Was quite expensive for a round comapred to other local places.

properties.name: Kelly & Coopers
properties.review: Borderline restaurant but was drinking room at the bar. Had a nice enough garden out the back but nothing particularly run-backableto about the place.
